notes.anchor: from there, as you advise, I expect to be at Ficulea
notes.note: Ficulea was a small town on the Via Nomentana northeast of Rome, where Atticus had an estate; Cicero plans to break his journey there. The place has no separate entity record in this corpus.

notes.anchor: what particular plot he wants to be cut out of the sale
notes.note: Silius wished to withhold (detrahere) some portion of the gardens from the sale; Cicero warns that the reserved plot may be the very part he wants for Tullia's shrine. The whole purchase was contemplated for that single piece of ground.

notes.anchor: I have sent you Hirtius' letter, both fresh and kindly written
notes.note: Aulus Hirtius, a leading Caesarian and consul-designate, had written warmly to Cicero, who forwards the letter to Atticus. Cultivating such men mattered both for consolation and for the Faberian financing of the gardens.
